sections.heading: RPE Calculator: What It Is and How to Use It
sections.content: An RPE (Rate of Perceived Exertion) Calculator helps measure workout intensity and estimate your strength. By entering weight lifted, repetitions, and perceived effort, it calculates your estimated one-rep max (1RM) and suggests recommended training loads. It’s ideal for fitness enthusiasts, lifters, powerlifters, and trainers looking to train safely, effectively, and progressively.

sections.heading: How to Use the RPE Calculator

subSections.heading: Enter Weight Lifted
subSections.content: Input the weight of your set (kg or lbs).

subSections.heading: Enter Repetitions
subSections.content: Number of reps completed for the set.

subSections.heading: Select RPE
subSections.content: Rate how hard the set felt on a scale from 1–10.

subSections.heading: Click Calculate
subSections.content: View estimated 1RM and recommended training loads.

subSections.heading: Adjust Workouts
subSections.content: Use the results to plan intensity and progression.

subSections.heading: 1RM Estimation
subSections.content: Converts weight, reps, and RPE into estimated maximum strength.

subSections.heading: Load Recommendations
subSections.content: Suggests weights for target intensity levels.

subSections.heading: Autoregulated Training
subSections.content: Adjust daily workouts based on perceived effort.

subSections.heading: Free & Mobile-Friendly
subSections.content: Works on any device without registration.

subSections.heading: Safe Training
subSections.content: Avoid unnecessary max lifts while tracking progress.

subSections.heading: Powerlifting
subSections.content: Estimate max lifts safely without going to failure.

subSections.heading: Autoregulated Workouts
subSections.content: Adjust daily loads based on effort.

subSections.heading: Coaching
subSections.content: Trainers can plan and monitor client loads accurately.

subSections.heading: Rehabilitation & Recovery
subSections.content: Manage stress to prevent injury and overtraining.

subSections.heading: Example 1
subSections.content: Weight: 100 kg | Reps: 5 | RPE: 8 → Estimated 1RM: 118 kg

subSections.heading: Example 2
subSections.content: Weight: 120 kg | Reps: 3 | RPE: 9 → Estimated 1RM: 129 kg

faq.a: It estimates strength and workout intensity using weight, repetitions, and perceived exertion.
No, it works directly in any browser.
Yes, based on the weight, reps, and RPE you enter.
Yes, it recommends training loads for target RPE levels.
Yes, it works for all fitness levels.
It’s accurate when inputs are consistent and correct.
Yes, by avoiding unnecessary max lifts and overtraining.

faq.q: Does it support kg and lbs?
Can coaches use it for clients?
faq.a: Yes, it’s perfect for monitoring and planning client workouts.
